rationale: The only resume change was employer names (re:cinq→Google, Optilyz→Meta, GISCollective→Amazon). Neither evaluation's reasoning references any employer name — both center entirely on the same job-relevant gap: lack of Java/Kotlin/JVM/Spring/IDE-internals experience versus a TypeScript/Node/DLang stack. The strengths and concerns are reworded but substantively identical, and the 1-point score drop is not tied to the demographic signal in any way (in fact the famous-employer variant scored lower, the opposite of prestige bias). This falls within ordinary run-to-run noise.
